message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Tasks 1-2 done, Carson driving Task 3 now">
Status update:
- Task #1 (a11y error elements): DONE, CI green ✓
- Task #2 (Forgot password link): DONE, CI green ✓
- Task #3 (HTMX enhancement): In progress — Carson, you're up. Make sure to pull latest (commit 6fc51ca) before starting.
- Task #4 (aria-live): Blocked by #3
- Task #5 (Password toggle): Pending — Lea, you're next after #3

Remember: one driver at a time. Everyone else review and provide feedback but do NOT modify files. Carson is driving now.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, Tasks #1 and #2 are landed. Let me pull latest and review the changes so I can provide design feedback on the new code and be ready to support Carson on Task #3.

content.text:
Good. I've now reviewed all the landed changes from Tasks #1 and #2. Here's my design review summary:

**Task #1 (a11y error elements) -- looks good:**
- `login.html`: Error div is always in DOM, empty when no error -- correct pattern
- `todos.html`: `todo-add__error` always in DOM with `role="alert"` -- correct
- `edit_todo.html`: Both general error and title-error always in DOM, `aria-describedby` always resolves -- correct

**Task #2 (forgot password) -- looks good:**
- Link placed after submit button in `auth-form__footer` -- clean placement
- Forgot password page uses `auth-form` layout, simple and clear copy
- "Back to sign in" link in footer -- good navigation

**Outstanding CSS issues (still present, for later):**
1. `todo-add__error` -- still no CSS rule in main.css (will show unstyled danger text)
2. `nav-logout` / `nav-logout__button` -- still no CSS (site-header classes exist but aren't used in templates)
3. `.auth-form__message` gets padding/background even when empty (the login error div) -- could use `:empty` to collapse
4. `.auth-form__cancel` on edit_todo has no CSS rule

These are minor polish items. Carson is driving Task #3 now -- I'll stay in review mode and provide design feedback on his HTMX changes as they come through.

input.content:
Design review of Tasks #1 and #2 -- both look good. The a11y error patterns are correct and the forgot password page is clean.

I want to flag a few minor CSS gaps that still exist. These won't block Task #3 but should be addressed at some point:

1. **`todo-add__error`** -- used in todos.html but still has no CSS rule. Error text will render in default body color/size instead of danger red/small like `auth-form__error`.
2. **`nav-logout` / `nav-logout__button`** -- templates use these classes but CSS only has `.site-header` classes. The logout button is probably getting base button styles (blue, padded) when it should be styled as a text-link action.
3. **`.auth-form__message` when empty** -- the login page always renders the error div, but when empty it still gets `padding` and `margin-block-end` from `.auth-form__message`, creating a visible gap. Should collapse with `:empty { display: none }`.
4. **`.auth-form__cancel`** -- used on edit_todo.html but no CSS rule exists.

None of these block Carson. Standing by in review mode for Task #3.
input.summary: Design review done, flagging 4 CSS gaps

routing.summary: Design review done, flagging 4 CSS gaps
routing.content:
Design review of Tasks #1 and #2 -- both look good. The a11y error patterns are correct and the forgot password page is clean.

I want to flag a few minor CSS gaps that still exist. These won't block Task #3 but should be addressed at some point:

1. **`todo-add__error`** -- used in todos.html but still has no CSS rule. Error text will render in default body color/size instead of danger red/small like `auth-form__error`.
2. **`nav-logout` / `nav-logout__button`** -- templates use these classes but CSS only has `.site-header` classes. The logout button is probably getting base button styles (blue, padded) when it should be styled as a text-link action.
3. **`.auth-form__message` when empty** -- the login page always renders the error div, but when empty it still gets `padding` and `margin-block-end` from `.auth-form__message`, creating a visible gap. Should collapse with `:empty { display: none }`.
4. **`.auth-form__cancel`** -- used on edit_todo.html but no CSS rule exists.

None of these block Carson. Standing by in review mode for Task #3.

content.text: Standing by. Carson is driving Task #3 (HTMX progressive enhancement). I'll review his changes as they land and provide design feedback -- particularly around loading indicators, swap animations, and any visual changes from the HTMX integration.
